headline: Survey at 18% completion; strong teacher engagement but critical regional and respondent gaps.
collection_status: 248 of 1,350 responses collected (18.4% of target). Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 162 responses (65% of current data); eight regions active but Singida severely underrepresented with only 1 response. Current pace suggests significant catch-up required to meet target.
key_findings: Teacher-led survey: 88 lower-primary and 33 upper-primary KiuFunza teachers comprise 49% of responses; head teachers add 22%, indicating strong buy-in from classroom level
Expansion is the priority: 89 respondents want KiuFunza expansion, far exceeding new programs (53) and policy work (53); consolidation (strengthen_kf: 47) ranks fourth
Partner satisfaction high (4.5/5) but scaling readiness low (2.8/5)—partners support the model but perceive constraints in systems, resources, or capacity
Feedback signals demand for incentives, digital monitoring, inter-stakeholder communication, government policy integration, and expansion of complementary programs (WiGo, SOMA)
flags: Regional imbalance: Singida (1), Shinyanga (16), and Songwe (18) lag severely; risk of skewed findings and incomplete regional coverage by survey close
Respondent skew: Only 2 non-KiuFunza teachers and 1 regional official captured; limited external/comparison perspective and weak regional-level voice in data
Scale readiness gap: Low readiness score (2.8/5) contradicts high expansion demand (89)—underlying barriers (staffing, funding, systems) not yet articulated in open feedback
recommendation: Immediately target Singida, Shinyanga, Songwe, and Kigoma with focused field efforts and incentivize regional/district education officers' participation to reach 500+ responses by mid-survey cycle. Concurrently, conduct rapid barrier analysis with 15–20 low-readiness respondents to translate expansion demand into actionable scaling constraints.
